title: Contact Me - Louise Haigh MP - Louise Haigh MP
text_excerpt: Contact Me - Louise Haigh MP - Louise Haigh MP Skip to main content Louise Haigh MP Fighting for Sheffield's future Home About Louise News Policy Campaigns In Parliament Contact Me Local Business Champions 2025 Cost-of-Living – help and support Mental Health Services Gleadless Valley Masterplan More Search Search the website Search Menu Contact Me Thank you so much for getting in touch with me. I receive hundreds of emails, letters and phone calls each day and my team do our very best to respond as quickly as possible, but the volume is growing. I aim to respond to all correspondence from constituents as swiftly as possible. Based on our current casework volumes, we aim to respond to you within ten working days and emergency cases will be prioritised. Constituents first: Due to the volume of emails I receive and the need to prioritise urgent casework, we don’t have capacity to respond to emails that aren’t relevant with my role as Member of Parliament for Sheffield Heeley. Checklist: Please check you are one of my constituents: There is a strict parliamentary protocol that MPs do not pursue issues raised by or about constituents of other MPs. So, before my team can process your enquiry, please check that you are one of my constituents. You can do this by entering your postcode here. You should also ensure that you have: Provided your full name. Provided your full postal address including post code. Provided a contact telephone number and an email address if you have one. Provided your national insurance number (where relevant) Attached copies of any supporting documents or media (where relevant). IMPORTANT: If you have not included this information, please re-send your email as I am unable to process your enquiry without it. Contact Details You can get in touch with me by post, phone, email, or by using the form below. If your issue is urgent then please call us on the phone. Address: House of Commons, London, SW1A 0AA Office hours: The office is open Monday to Friday from 9am to 5pm, but please ring ahead before visiting. Constituency Phone number: 0114 250 8113 Contact Email address: louise.haigh.mp@parliament.uk Twitter: twitter.com/LouHaigh Facebook: facebook.com/LouiseHaighMP LinkedIN: www.linkedin.com/in/louise-haigh-mp-8a5362282 How I can help I can help constituents with the following issues: Tax problems involving the HM Revenue and Customs Department Welfare issues dealt with by the Department for Work and Pensions such as benefits, tax credits and state pensions. Issues handled by the Home Office such as immigration and passports. Issues relating to the Department of Health & Social Care such as concerns about hospitals or access to treatment. Any issues arising from any other government departments. Issues with utility companies. Consumer issues. You have a policy issue that you want me to raise with the Government I can support constituents by: Writing to the Minister with responsibility for the department concerned. Asking an official for an explanation of why a decision has been made or policy position taken. Asking for a decision to be reviewed. Help with council issues IMPORTANT: Issues relating to services provided by Sheffield Council need to be raised with the relevant council department. This includes: Housing and council tax Anti-social behaviour Highways and roads Planning Bins and littering School placements Adults’ and children’s social services You can find out more about council services here. Invitations: I receive many invitations and we try to offer apologies where I am unable to attend, and I prioritise invitations that are relevant to my constituents. As an MP my diary reacts to urgent issues and on occasion I may need to postpone or cancel meetings or attending an event, sometimes at short notice. Where possible my team will try and rearrange. Please accept my apologies if you don’t receive a response to an event invite within two weeks. Policy If you have contacted me about a policy related is
